summary: Matched a large portion of the fighter dynamics module by moving the old ftCommon/ftCo_09CB work into a new src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c and ftdynamics.h, deleting the old ftCo_09CB.c/.h and assembly file, and updating build/config symbol ownership. The PR reported 71.50% matched for ftdynamics.c, with many smaller functions at 100% and larger routines such as ftCo_8009CB40 and ftCo_8009E7B4 still unmatched. It also renamed the 0x8009E0D4 function to ftCo_UnloadDynamicBones and propagated improved dynamic-bone type names through fighter, collision, item, ground, and library code.
what_changed: Added src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c with 725 lines implementing many fighter dynamic-bone routines, including ftCo_8009CF84, multiple Kirby-hat dynamic initializers, ftCo_8009DD94, ftCo_UnloadDynamicBones, ftCo_8009E140, ftCo_8009E1D4, ftCo_8009E318, ftCo_8009E4A8, ftCo_8009E614, ftCo_8009E714, and ftCo_8009EAF8, while leaving large routines such as ftCo_8009CB40 and ftCo_8009E7B4 as unmatched placeholders.
Deleted src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_09CB.c and ftCo_09CB.h, replacing their public header role with src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.h.
Deleted asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_09CB.s and added asm/melee/ft/ftdynamics.s, aligning the object/module name with ftdynamics rather than ftCommon/ftCo_09CB.
Renamed ftCo_8009E0D4 to ftCo_UnloadDynamicBones and updated callsites, including Kirby initialization assembly references and symbol configuration.

Changed Fighter.dynamic_bone_sets from the local FtDynamicBones type to BoneDynamicsDesc dynamic_bone_sets[Ft_Dynamics_NumMax], with member use changing from x4_dynamicBones/x0_apply_phys_num to dyn_desc/bone_id.

Recovered and renamed dynamic data structures: ArticleDynamicBones now stores BoneDynamicsDesc entries, ftDynamics exposes dynamicsNum and ftDynamicBones plus x10 as Fighter_Part*, and KirbyHatStruct now has named fields such as hat_joint, joint_num, hat_vis_table, and hat_dynamics[5].
Updated ftData layout around x28/x2C/x30 to expose struct ftDynamics* x2C.
Corrected FighterBone flag offsets and size by moving bitfield comments from +5 to +8 and replacing filler with xC, with a STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct FighterBone) == 0x10).
Renamed Fighter.frame_spd_mul to frame_speed_mul and updated many callsites.
Replaced lb_UnkAnimStruct with DynamicsDesc in bury, collision, ground, and related APIs, changing uses of x4_size to count.
Adjusted many stage callback signatures returning dynamic descriptors from lb_UnkAnimStruct* to DynamicsDesc*.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt and scratches.txt with ftdynamics symbols, including making some string/data symbols local and recording ftCo_UnloadDynamicBones.
smart_moves: Moved the code into a dedicated ft/ftdynamics module instead of continuing to treat it as ftCommon/ftCo_09CB, which makes future dynamic-bone work searchable and structurally closer to the original object layout.
Introduced ftCo_UnloadDynamicBones as a meaningful name for the small fully matched unload/free routine at 0x8009E0D4, then propagated that name to callsites and symbols.
Used recovered structure names and field names to make the dynamic-bone logic readable: BoneDynamicsDesc, DynamicsDesc, dynamicsNum, ftDynamicBones, dyn_desc, bone_id, and count.
Preserved matching progress pragmatically by leaving large difficult routines as assembly/unmatched while decompiling many small and medium-sized functions to C.
Added explicit size/layout checks where type recovery affected core fighter layout, notably STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct FighterBone) == 0x10).
Propagated DynamicsDesc through related systems rather than keeping a misleading lb_UnkAnimStruct placeholder in stage/collision APIs.
decomp_lessons: When a file is discovered to be a standalone module, move it to its canonical module path and update headers, object lists, splits, and symbols together; this PR migrated ftCommon/ftCo_09CB into ft/ftdynamics.
Renaming a small, well-understood function such as ftCo_UnloadDynamicBones can clarify many callsites and reduce future ambiguity even if adjacent large routines remain unmatched.

Dynamic-bone matching depends heavily on accurate shared structure names and field offsets; improving types in ft/types.h and lb/types.h can unblock matching in fighter, stage, collision, and item code simultaneously.
Use the data format itself to guide names: x4_size became count in DynamicsDesc, dynamic_bone_sets entries became BoneDynamicsDesc, and ftDynamics gained dynamicsNum/ftDynamicBones fields.

For repeated character-specific initialization routines, matching often comes from identifying the same traversal pattern over HSD_JObj child/next/parent chains plus per-character table indices such as hat_dynamics[n].
When changing struct field names used across many files, update all dependent signatures and return types in the same PR to avoid placeholder-type drift.
Keep evidence of nonmatched functions clear in the progress report; this PR reported exact function scores and did not imply full module completion.
naming_conventions: Use module header src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.h for ftdynamics declarations rather than a chara/ftCommon/ftCo_09CB.h header.
Name the 0x8009E0D4 routine ftCo_UnloadDynamicBones when it frees/unloads each dynamic_bone_sets[i].dyn_desc and resets dynamics_num to 0.
Prefer DynamicsDesc over lb_UnkAnimStruct for dynamic/collision descriptor data, and use count rather than x4_size for the descriptor element count.
Prefer BoneDynamicsDesc for per-bone dynamic descriptor entries and dyn_desc/bone_id for its fields instead of x4_dynamicBones/x0_apply_phys_num.
Use frame_speed_mul rather than frame_spd_mul for Fighter animation speed multiplier.
Use KirbyHatStruct field names hat_joint, joint_num, hat_vis_table, and hat_dynamics instead of x0_kirbyHatJointPtr, x4_jointNum, x8_hatVisTable, and x14_hatDynamics.
Use ftDynamics fields dynamicsNum and ftDynamicBones for the dynamic table header.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Kept ftCo_8009CB40 and ftCo_8009E7B4 as unmatched/assembly-backed where the report still showed 0.00%, while matching many smaller routines to reach 71.50% for the file.
Used PAD_STACK in functions where stack shape was needed for matching, including ftCo_8009DB50 and ftCo_8009E140.
Used ssize_t loop indices in many dynamic-bone loops, matching signed comparisons and iteration over dynamics_num.
Used goto labels in ftCo_8009E318 to preserve control flow for the two dynamic-bone toggle exits.
Made string/data symbols such as ftDynamics_803C5720 local in config/symbol updates where the new C/asm organization did not require global exposure.
Kept assert strings and line numbers in ftdynamics.c evidence-grounded, including __FILE__ assert use and HSD_ASSERT in one routine.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice, so there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match ftCo_8009CB40 or ftCo_8009E7B4 in src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c.
Search for additional renames or layout fixes to DynamicsDesc, BoneDynamicsDesc, ArticleDynamicBones, and ftDynamics after PR 1432.
Check whether the static declarations in ftdynamics.h for internal ftCo_8009D18C-style routines were later moved into C-local declarations or exposed differently.
Look for subsequent PRs touching frame_speed_mul and FighterBone flags to confirm the naming/layout stabilized.
